headerDisplay: Ambient Weather plug-in for [Homebridge](https://github.com/peledies/homebridge-ambient-weather-sensors) using the HTTP Rest API

name.title: Name
name.description: The name that will appear in your homebridge log

apiKey.title: API Key
apiKey.placeholder: api key from ambient weather account
apiKey.description: Stored in plain text in Homebridge's config.json. Treat the file with the same care as any password-bearing config.

applicationKey.title: Application Key
applicationKey.placeholder: application key from ambient weather account
applicationKey.description: Stored in plain text in Homebridge's config.json. Treat the file with the same care as any password-bearing config.

co2Sensors.title: CO2 Sensors
co2Sensors.description: Expose CO2 readings from AWN's AQIN family (co2_in_aqin) and any standalone CO2 sensor.

airQualitySensors.title: Air Quality Sensors (PM2.5 / PM10)
airQualitySensors.description: Expose particulate readings (pm25, pm25_in_aqin, pm10_in_aqin) as HomeKit AirQualitySensor accessories with the corresponding density characteristic and an EPA-bucket-derived AirQuality enum.

dataSource.title: Data Source
dataSource.description: Polling fetches the AWN REST endpoint every 2 minutes. Realtime opens a websocket to rt2.ambientweather.net and receives updates as the station reports them (typically ~30s indoors). Realtime is opt-in for now; the default will switch to realtime in a future release once it has been broadly validated.

excludeSensors.title: Exclude Sensors (blacklist)
excludeSensors.description: Hide specific sensors. Matching is case-insensitive and whitespace is trimmed, so typos like trailing spaces from copy-paste still work. Each entry can be a sensor's friendly name ("Indoor Temperature"), a station name to hide everything from that station ("Backyard Station"), a raw AWN field ("tempinf"), a MAC address, or the full uniqueId ("AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF-tempinf"). The per-type toggles above still apply on top of this list.

includeOnly.title: Include Only These Sensors (allowlist)
includeOnly.description: If set, ONLY sensors matching one of these entries are exposed; everything else is hidden. Same matching rules as Exclude Sensors above (case-insensitive, whitespace-trimmed, accepts friendly names / station names / raw AWN fields / MAC / uniqueId). Useful when you have multiple stations and only want HomeKit to see specific ones. Both filters apply: a sensor must match an allowlist entry AND not match a blacklist entry to be exposed.
